subject: Project Management
title: Henry Gantt's Chart
description: A micro-lesson that makes Henry Gantt's Chart usable.

hook.text: In 1910, Henry Gantt drew a bar chart showing tasks against time. It changed PM forever.

buildup.text: Before Gantt, project schedules were written in prose. Nobody could see the whole plan.

discovery.text: His chart showed start dates, durations, and overlaps — a project made visual.

twist.text: The U.S. Army used Gantt charts to coordinate WWI logistics — shipping and troop movements.

climax.text: Over 100 years later, every tool from MS Project to Jira still uses Gantt's basic idea.

punchline.text: The most powerful PM tool is a bar on a timeline.

quiz.question: What did the Gantt chart introduce to project management?
options: A visual representation of tasks against time
